question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum collection size check in validate(), passing a collection with exactly one VERIFIED signature whose node has stake >= stakeRequiredForConsensus, to authorize a transaction with only one signer when the network design requires 1/3 of all nodes to agree?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable nature of StreamFileSignature (Lombok @Data generates setters) to modify the fileHash of a signature after it has been added to signatureHashMap but before the stake-accumulation loop runs, causing the signature to be counted under a different hash key than the one it was verified against, leading to false consensus for a hash that was never actually signed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() sets CONSENSUS_REACHED status on signatures via validatedSignatures.forEach(s -> s.setStatus(CONSENSUS_REACHED)) even when those signatures were not individually cryptographically verified (their status was manually set to VERIFIED before calling validate()), causing downstream consumers that check for CONSENSUS_REACHED to trust unverified signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a collection where the ConsensusNode for each signature implements getTotalStake() to return different values per call (non-deterministic), causing the stakeRequiredForConsensus computed from the first call to be inconsistent with the actual network state, potentially lowering the threshold below the intended 1/3 and authorizing transactions without sufficient signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HashMultimap.create() usage, which does not enforce uniqueness of values per key beyond object equality, by submitting StreamFileSignature objects that are equal by EqualsAndHashCode (same filename + node) but have different stake values via a custom ConsensusNode, causing the stake loop to count both entries and inflate the apparent consensus stake?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not verify that the fileHash in each StreamFileSignature actually matches the content of the downloaded stream file, meaning an attacker who can inject VERIFIED-status signatures with an arbitrary fileHash can cause the validator to reach consensus on a hash that corresponds to a malicious file, authorizing transactions from that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a check that the signatures collection contains entries from at least N distinct nodes, allowing an attacker to submit many VERIFIED signatures all from a single high-stake node, satisfying the stake threshold with one signer and authorizing transactions that should require approval from multiple independent network participants?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stakeRequiredForConsensus is a BigDecimal computed with setScale(0, RoundingMode.CEILING), and for very small totalStake values (e.g., totalStake=1, consensusRatio=1/3), the ceiling rounds up to 1, meaning a single node with stake=1 satisfies canReachConsensus(), reducing the effective consensus requirement to a single signer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check whether the SignatureStatus.VERIFIED was set by the legitimate NodeSignatureVerifier or by an external caller, allowing any code path that can construct a StreamFileSignature and set its status to VERIFIED to bypass the RSA signature check and have transactions authorized without genuine node approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method iterates over signatureHashMap.keySet() and for each key sums stake independently, meaning an attacker who can split their forged signatures across multiple hash groups can ensure at least one group accumulates enough stake to pass canReachConsensus(), even if no single legitimate hash group has sufficient stake?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not validate the SignatureType of each StreamFileSignature, allowing an attacker to inject signatures with a null or unexpected SignatureType that bypasses the RSA verification in NodeSignatureVerifier (which would return false and leave status as DOWNLOADED), but if the attacker pre-sets status to VERIFIED, the validate() method still counts them toward consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not enforce that the collection is non-null before iterating, and if a null element is present in the collection, the code at signature.getNode().getTotalStake() would throw a NullPointerException, causing the validation to abort with an unchecked exception rather than a SignatureVerificationException, potentially disrupting the importer's error handling and allowing a retry with a manipulated collection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method reads totalStake from the first signature's node only once and caches it in a local variable, while subsequent signatures may belong to nodes with different totalStake values (inconsistent network state), allowing an attacker to front-load a low-totalStake node to set a low threshold and then include high-stake VERIFIED signatures from other nodes to easily meet that threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check whether the filename extracted from the first signature matches the filenames of all other signatures in the collection, allowing an attacker to mix signatures from a legitimate high-consensus file with a malicious file's signatures, causing the malicious file's hash group to benefit from the legitimate file's totalStake context?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not verify that the metadataHash and metadataHashSignature fields are consistent with the fileHash, allowing an attacker to inject a VERIFIED-status signature with a legitimate fileHash but a malicious metadataHash, causing the validator to grant CONSENSUS_REACHED to a signature whose metadata was never properly verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check the version field of StreamFileSignature, allowing an attacker to inject signatures with a version that triggers different behavior in downstream consumers (e.g., hasCompressedDataFile()), while the consensus check passes normally, leading to processing of a malicious file under incorrect version assumptions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not enforce a maximum collection size, allowing an attacker to submit an extremely large collection of VERIFIED signatures (e.g., millions of entries), causing the HashMultimap to consume excessive heap memory and triggering an OutOfMemoryError that disrupts the importer service, preventing legitimate transactions from being validated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check whether the ConsensusNode returned by signature.getNode() is null, causing a NullPointerException at signature.getNode().getTotalStake() that bypasses the SignatureVerificationException path and may be caught by a broader exception handler that retries or skips validation, leading to unauthorized transaction authorization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not verify that the fileHashAsHex() values in the signatureHashMap correspond to the actual content of the downloaded stream file, allowing an attacker to inject VERIFIED signatures with a pre-chosen fileHash that matches a malicious file, causing the validator to authorize that malicious file as if it had network consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check whether the same node appears in multiple hash groups (i.e., a node signing two different file hashes), allowing an attacker to use a single compromised node's stake to contribute to multiple hash groups simultaneously, potentially enabling a forged hash group to reach consensus by borrowing stake from a legitimate group?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not enforce that the collection contains signatures from a minimum number of distinct nodes, allowing an attacker to submit a collection with only one node's signature (even if that node has >= 1/3 of total stake), satisfying the stake-based consensus check while bypassing the intended multi-party approval requirement?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check whether the StreamFilename extracted from the first signature is null, causing a NullPointerException in the error message format string (String.format(..., filename, ...)) if filename remains null (empty collection edge case), disrupting the exception handling path and potentially masking the real validation failure?
